---
title: "Teaching"
site: distill::distill_website
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE)
```

```{css, echo = FALSE}
/* Shrink the main page title */
d-title h1 {
  font-size: 2.2em; 
}

d-title, d-article {
  grid-template-columns: [screen-start] 1fr [text-start kicker-end] 2% 2% 2% 2% 2% 2% 2% 2% [text-end gutter-start] 2% [middle-end] 2% [page-end gutter-end] 1fr [screen-end];
  grid-column-gap: 10%;
}

body .title, body .article {
  max-width: 100%; /* Increase max-width to take more space */
}

/* Additional CSS for side-by-side layout */
.content-section {
  display: flex;
  justify-content: space-between; /* or 'space-around' to include some space between columns */
  align-items: flex-start;
}

.left-column {
  width: 50%; /* Adjust width as necessary */
}

.right-column {
  width: 50%; /* Adjust width as necessary */
}

.right-align-header {
  text-align: right;
}

/* Optional: if you want to ensure there is a little gap between the columns */
.content-section > div {
  padding: 0 10px;
}
```

I'm the recipient of <b> 2026 Outstanding Graduate Student Instructor Award </b> at UC Berkeley.

### Teaching Experience

Graduate Student Instructor for ECON 201B: Economic Theory (PhD level) | UC Berkeley | Spring 2026

Graduate Student Instructor for ECON 101A: Microeconomics (Quantitative) | UC Berkeley | Fall 2025

Reader for ECON C103: Introduction to Mathematical Economics | UC Berkeley | Fall 2024 and Spring 2025

Teaching Assistant for MTH 254: Optimization Theory | Wake Forest University | Fall 2021
